description_long: A cluster of four mushrooms is growing from a circular patch of textured brown earth. The largest mushroom features a wide, domed red cap adorned with numerous white spots, and a thick, cylindrical stem. Three smaller mushrooms, each with similarly colored caps and white spots, are grouped closely around the base of the main stem, with two of them leaning outward and one nestled near the center. The caps have a smooth, matte finish and the white spots are irregularly distributed. The stems are pale beige and slightly tapered, emerging directly from the soil. The overall appearance is stylized and vibrant, reminiscent of the iconic Amanita muscaria, and the mushrooms are depicted as healthy and robust.
description: A vibrant cluster of four red-capped, white-spotted mushrooms with beige stems growing from a textured brown patch of earth. The largest mushroom stands centrally, surrounded by three smaller ones.
description_view_0: The main large mushroom cap dominates the view, with two smaller mushrooms visible at the front and one partially hidden behind.
description_view_1: All four mushrooms are clearly visible, with the two smaller mushrooms on the right side leaning outward and the smallest one in the center.
description_view_2: The large cap is prominent, with the smaller mushrooms mostly grouped to the left and partially obscured by the main cap.
description_view_3: The rear of the cluster is shown, with the stems of the mushrooms more visible and the caps overlapping from this angle.
